title: Bank of America Is Being Priced for Problems It Isn't Having
detail:
Bank of America Is Being Priced for Problems It Isn't Having
Sheraden Frances
Tue, March 17, 2026 at 11:38 AM CDT 13 min read
In this article:
BAC

This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

When I look at the current state of the banking sector, I see a classic disconnect between fear and reality, and nowhere is this more apparent than with Bank of America. The stock market is currently punishing the company based on political headlines and fears of regulatory changes, yet the actual business is performing significantly better than anyone expected. I believe we are witnessing a setup for a sharp, V-shaped recovery in the share price because the underlying engine of the bank is firing on all cylinders. The recent fourth-quarter earnings report for the fiscal year 2025 was not just good; it was a clear demonstration that the bank has built a fortress capable of withstanding the exact headwinds the market is worried about. While traders are selling based on what might happen in Washington, I am looking at the cold, hard cash the bank is generating right now.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 3 Warning Sign with BAC.

Is BAC fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

Bank of America Is Being Priced for Problems It Isn't Having

BAC Data by GuruFocus

My conviction in this trade comes from a simple observation: the narrative has become too negative relative to the numbers. We have a company that just beat analyst expectations on both the top and bottom lines, yet the stock price has dipped, offering what I consider to be a gift to patient investors. The fear revolves around potential caps on credit card interest rates and the future path of Fed policy, but when I dig into the balance sheet, I see a company that is growing loans, expanding margins, and managing credit risk better than almost any of its peers. The sell-off feels emotional, driven by knee-jerk reactions to news bites rather than a sober analysis of earnings power. In my view, the fundamentals are intact, the guidance for 2026 is solid, and the valuation has compressed to a level that provides a massive margin of safety.

I am approaching this as a strong buying opportunity because the market is ignoring the resilience of the American consumer and Bank of America's specific strengths. The bank is not just a lender; it also has a booming investment banking division and a massive wealth management arm. The market seems to be focusing too heavily on one or two regulatory risks. I see a path to significant capital appreciation over the next twelve months as the political noise fades and the market is forced to acknowledge the bank's growing earnings power. I am not trying to time the exact bottom, but I am confident that it will play out well in the long run.

title: Why USD is the only safe haven in FX
detail:
Why USD is the only safe haven in FX
Sam Boughedda
Tue, March 17, 2026 at 10:24 AM CDT 1 min read
In this article:
BAC
DX-Y.NYB

Investing.com -- The U.S. dollar is being seen as the sole reliable refuge in global foreign-exchange markets as geopolitical risks rise, according to a new note from Bank of America.

Analysts led by Adarsh Sinha said quant models show investors are increasingly preparing for a “longer war and higher energy prices,” with market positioning shifting decisively toward the dollar.

BofA wrote that “USD should be the main beneficiary,” noting that bullish signals have been “triggered vs all other perceived ‘safe havens.’”

Option-flow data show “high demand for USD calls vs all other perceived ‘safe havens,’” reinforcing what the bank describes as a broad repricing across FX.

The bank said risks tied to the war in the Middle East are rising sharply.

“As the war in the Middle East enters its 3rd week, the risks of a more protracted conflict and permanently higher energy prices have increased meaningfully,” the analysts wrote, adding that markets had been “slow to price in such a scenario” but that repricing “is under way that should support further USD strength.”

BofA’s technical models now flag bearish continuation signals for the Swedish krona and New Zealand dollar.

The bank said SEK and NZD “tend to sell off on macro shock events,” and its “FX Signal of the Week” recommends bullish USD/SEK and bearish NZD/USD positions.

Even traditional safe havens are weakening. “EUR and JPY have also been in supported downtrends vs the USD for several weeks, and even the mighty CHF has lost its uptrend,” BofA said.

“Until de-escalation is clear,” the bank concluded, “it seems like USD will reign supreme.”

Related articles

detail:

Sheryar Siddiq
Tue, March 17, 2026 at 7:07 AM CDT 1 min read
In this article:
CELH
BAC
PEP

Celsius Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ:CELH) ranks among the best growth stocks to buy and hold for the long term. On February 27, Bank of America double upgraded Celsius Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ:CELH) to Buy from Underperform and boosted its price target to $65 from $45, citing better-than-expected fourth-quarter performance and stronger 2026 growth projections. According to BofA, Alani Nu drove fourth-quarter success, exceeding forecasts as it integrated into the PepsiCo network.

At the CAGNY conference, Celsius Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ:CELH) management announced 17% shelf space improvements for the core Celsius brand in North America by 2026. Although inventory changes in the second half of 2025 cause short-term volatility in comparisons, BofA stated that the increased distribution could promote consumption growth.

BofA stated that its previous Underperform rating was primarily motivated by valuation and momentary comparison constraints, in contrast to issues related to the brand or the overall energy drink category. The firm emphasized that non-alcoholic beverages retain their favored category within consumer staples.

Celsius Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ:CELH) develops, processes, manufactures, markets, sells, and distributes functional energy drinks in the US, North America, Europe, the Asia Pacific, and internationally.

title: Nu Holdings (NU) Revenue Surges 45% Year-Over-Year, Beats Expectations
detail:
Nu Holdings (NU) Revenue Surges 45% Year-Over-Year, Beats Expectations
Sheryar Siddiq
Tue, March 17, 2026 at 7:07 AM CDT 1 min read
In this article:
NU
BAC

Nu Holdings Ltd. (NYSE:NU) ranks among the best growth stocks to buy and hold for the long term. On March 9, BofA Securities reduced its price objective for Nu Holdings Ltd. (NYSE:NU) to $17 from $18, maintaining a Neutral rating. The firm decreased its target price-to-earnings multiple to 21x from 22x to take account of the de-rating associated with global high-growth banking companies.

Due to increased risk and operating costs, the company’s fourth-quarter 2025 earnings before taxes fell short of BofA projections by about 15%. That said, Nu Holdings Ltd. (NYSE:NU) announced revenue of $4.9 billion, surpassing average expectations of $3.79 billion by 29% and representing a 45% year-over-year jump.

BofA’s net income forecasts remain intact, with higher operating expenses and reserve charges for loan losses offset by stronger revenues and a lower effective tax rate. In that regard, the firm expects earnings growth of 45% in 2026 and 28% in 2027, with a return on equity of around 30%.

Nu Holdings Ltd. (NYSE:NU) provides a digital banking platform in Brazil, Mexico, Colombia, the Cayman Islands, and the US. It offers spending and mobile payment solutions, as well as Nu Shopping.

title: UK Bank of England expected to hold rates amid energy price surge
detail:
UK Bank of England expected to hold rates amid energy price surge
Maria Ponnezhath
Tue, March 17, 2026 at 6:16 AM CDT 3 min read
In this article:
BAC

Investing.com -- Bank of America expects the Bank of England to keep its Bank Rate unchanged in March as policymakers assess the impact of rising energy prices following the Iran conflict, according to a research note published Tuesday.

The bank forecasts UK growth of 1.2% in 2026 and 1.4% in 2027, with inflation at 2.2% and 2.0% respectively. However, the recent surge in energy prices has introduced stagflationary risks that could alter this outlook.

The size and persistence of the energy price shock will determine its ultimate impact on inflation, inflation expectations and the BoE’s response, analysts said. At current energy futures levels, inflation could run approximately 40 basis points higher in 2026 than previously forecast, with a peak increase of 60 basis points in the second half of the year.

Bank of America now expects two Bank Rate cuts in June and September, delayed from its previous forecast of March and June. The bank acknowledged this is a low conviction call, noting that an earlier cut remains possible but risks are tilted toward further delays or fewer cuts this year if the conflict persists.

The BoE is expected to vote 7-2 to hold rates in March, with risks of a 6-3 split. While maintaining an easing bias, the central bank is likely to add that uncertainty and upside inflation risks have increased, making judgements around further easing an even closer call.

Wage growth has shown encouraging signs of moderation, with 2026 pay settlements coming in at 3.4%, down from 4% in 2025. However, the BoE remains sensitive to potential second-round effects from the energy shock, though a weaker labour market reduces these risks.

The unemployment rate has risen to 5.2%, with survey measures pointing to weak employment intentions. The number of inactive workers remains 412,000 higher than 2019 levels.

Despite disinflationary Budget measures and pay moderation, the move higher in energy prices could delay the return to 2% inflation versus the second half of 2026 in current forecasts. In a quick resolution scenario, inflation would be 15-20 basis points higher in 2026. If the shock extends into the second quarter, inflation could run 40 basis points higher in 2026.

The bar for a rate hike remains high, analysts said, given that rates are restrictive, downside growth risks exist and the labour market is weaker compared to 2022.

In fixed income markets, the previously priced-in path of roughly 50 basis points of rate cuts in 2026 has been fully unwound, with the curve now pricing in a relatively high probability of a 25 basis point Bank Rate hike by year-end.

title: Bank of America Corporation (BAC) Presents at RBC Conference, Here’s What You Need to Know

Bank of America Corporation (NYSE:BAC) is one of the Most Undervalued Long Term Stocks to Buy According to Analysts. On March 10, Bank of America Corporation (NYSE:BAC) presented at the RBC Capital Markets Global Financial Institutions Conference. At the conference, Co-President Dean Athanasia noted the bank’s strategic priorities, robust financial performance, and optimistic outlook amid market volatility.

​Management highlighted that consumer spending remains strong at 5% to 6% year-over-year growth, especially in entertainment and travel, supporting a K-shaped economy where higher-income groups show faster wage and spending gains. Bank of America Corporation (NYSE:BAC) noted that its wealth management oversees $5.5 trillion in assets under management, and the bank targets 4% to 5% medium-term net new asset growth. This is  complemented by $600 billion in workplace benefits for 24,000 corporate clients.

​Notably, the bank spends $13 billion on technology annually, including $4 billion for new initiatives. These new initiatives include AI tools like the Erica assistant, handling over 3 billion transactions. Looking ahead, management expects Basel III Endgame proposals soon, which are expected to potentially ease capital requirements. Lastly, the bank is focused on maintaining a CET1 ratio of 11.4%.

​Bank of America Corporation (NYSE:BAC) delivers financial solutions to individuals, small and mid-sized enterprises, large institutions, and governments. It has a global presence with expertise in consumer banking, wealth & investment management, and capital markets. The company offers a range of financial products & services across its four broad segments.

title: Epstein Settlement Puts Bank Of America Valuation And Governance In Focus
detail:
Epstein Settlement Puts Bank Of America Valuation And Governance In Focus
Simply Wall St
Tue, March 17, 2026 at 4:09 AM CDT 3 min read
In this article:
BAC

Track your investments for FREE with Simply Wall St, the portfolio command center trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.

Bank of America, NYSE:BAC, has agreed to settle a lawsuit over its past relationship with Jeffrey Epstein and alleged failures to promptly report suspicious activities.

The settlement addresses claims that the bank maintained Epstein related accounts and delayed filing required suspicious activity reports.

This outcome brings a legal resolution to issues that raised questions about the bank's compliance controls and oversight of high risk clients.

For investors watching NYSE:BAC, this legal development sits alongside a mixed recent share price record. The stock is currently at $47.06, with a 16.1% gain over the past year and an 83.3% return over three years. However, a 41.1% return over five years and a 15.9% decline year to date highlight a more uneven picture. These numbers frame the settlement as another factor that could influence how the market views the bank's risk profile and governance.

The settlement may prompt closer attention to Bank of America's compliance practices and how it manages relationships with higher risk clients. Readers may want to watch for any additional regulatory follow up, changes in internal controls, or commentary from management that could shape confidence in NYSE:BAC over time.

title: Why consumers love, and sometimes hate, tiered loyalty programs
detail:
Why consumers love, and sometimes hate, tiered loyalty programs
Bryan Wassel
Tue, March 17, 2026 at 2:00 AM CDT 5 min read
In this article:
BAC
SBUX

This story was originally published on CX Dive. To receive daily news and insights, subscribe to our free daily CX Dive newsletter.

Tiered loyalty programs are popular among businesses and customers alike, with companies like Bank of America and Starbucks each moving to tiered structures this spring.

The love for tiers isn’t a surprise. Tiered loyalty programs drive aspiration, which taps into humanity’s shared love of doing better and getting more, according to Patricia Camden, managing director of EY Studio+ Customer Experience and loyalty leader.

“We're guided by visible progress,” Camden told CX Dive. “I like to think of it like when a customer sees a ladder. You just want to climb that ladder, man. You want to move up.”

Tiers offer multiple benefits to a loyalty program, including a sense of exclusivity, the ability to offer flexible benefits and extra opportunities for brands to engage with customers. Conversely, poorly implemented tier systems can feel frustratingly slow or complex.

Many customers love tiers and will join any program that offers them, according to Camden. EY’s research finds that the customer preference for tiered loyalty models is growing — but companies still need to make sure their loyalty program is working for them.

Tiers can make customers feel special

Tiers can be a great way to make every customer feel special while reserving the most expensive benefits for shoppers whose spending warrants such investment.

By rewarding top-spending customers, companies reinforce the idea of status and recognition for customers who spend a lot of time and money with a brand.

Earlier this month, Michaels revamped its loyalty program with an additional tier for its biggest spending customers. This change was “about recognizing and rewarding our most dedicated shoppers,” Michaels President and Chief Customer Officer Heather Bennett said.

But that sense of exclusivity isn’t reserved for customers who spend hundreds or thousands with a given brand every year, according to Camden. Even smaller benefits at lower tiers can make the loyalty relationship feel special.

“Customers love feeling like they're part of an exclusive group, and even tiny benefits like priority service or free shipping or early access can feel really, really meaningful,” Camden said.

Brands can also influence customer behavior with how often tiers reset, according to Bobby Stephens, principal in Deloitte Digital’s retail and consumer products practice. Depending on the brand, it might make sense for a tier to be a lifetime achievement, or it may be better for customers to need to reach a certain annual spend to retain their status.

title: BofA urges investors to buy these 3 sectors, not the S&P 500
detail:
BofA urges investors to buy these 3 sectors, not the S&P 500
Sam Boughedda
Mon, March 16, 2026 at 10:20 AM CDT 1 min read
In this article:
BAC
XLY
CL
^GSPC
CL=F

Investing.com -- Bank of America said in a note Monday that clients should favour energy, consumer staples, and large-cap value stocks over the broader S&P 500, arguing that key risks are not fully reflected in the benchmark’s valuation.

Equity and quant strategist Savita Subramanian wrote that investors should “buy Energy/Staples/LCV, not the index.”

According to Bank of America, the relationship between the S&P 500 and crude oil prices suggests the market remains stretched.

Subramanian said a simple ratio analysis shows the index “is still trading higher, in WTI terms, than any other point in history … except COVID” and the 2000 tech bubble.

On taxes, BofA argued that stronger receipts are already priced in, supporting some discretionary names, but warned that a “higher bill for short term equity gains” is not.

Subramanian recommended selling discretionary stocks.

Consumer staples, by contrast, appear better positioned, and while lower demand from net migration is reflected in valuations, Subramanian said tighter labour markets could buoy wages and support spending, noting that this backdrop encourages a “trade up in food, personal products and other goods.”

The bank also highlighted cash dynamics, saying retirees’ money-market holdings are unlikely to support technology dips, while institutional cash balances are at five-year lows.

Subramanian wrote that a rotation within the index “necessitates selling existing holdings,” reinforcing the case for large-cap value.

title: Why PayPal’s Rally Faded—And What Could Restart It
detail:
Why PayPal’s Rally Faded—And What Could Restart It
Sam Quirke, MarketBeat
Mon, March 16, 2026 at 10:11 AM CDT 5 min read
In this article:
PYPL
MOVE
BAC
Customer holding smartphone displaying PayPal app at a checkout counter, highlighting digital payments platform.
Key Points

PayPal shares popped last month after takeover speculation sparked renewed investor interest, but the rally is already losing momentum.

The stock remains near multi-year lows and trades at one of its lowest valuations ever, suggesting there could be meaningful upside if sentiment shifts.

However, for PayPal to maintain a rally, the company will need to impress investors with its next earnings report and prove its long-term relevance in digital payments.

Interested in PayPal Holdings, Inc.? Here are five stocks we like better.

PayPal Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: PYPL) appeared to be staging a long-awaited comeback at the end of February. After months of relentless selling that pushed the stock back to 2017 levels, shares surged following rumors that payments giant Stripe was exploring an acquisition.

The speculation was enough to ignite a powerful rally, with PayPal shares popping as much as 25% from their multi-year lows. However, once it became clear that the takeover speculation had no substance, much of that enthusiasm quickly faded. It retraced 10% in mid-March and looks poised to slide back toward its February lows. For the rally to regain momentum from here, the company has to do several things very well—let’s take a closer look at what.

→ Data Storage to Data Intelligence: Everpure's Big AI Era Rebrand

Its Next Earnings Report Will Be Key

One of the biggest catalysts for PayPal’s next move will be its next earnings report in early May. After a difficult start to the year, the company needs to show investors that its growth story is not just in terminal decline.

Hopefully, the bar to success will be low. Last month, PayPal reported results that fell well short of expectations, disappointing investors who had hoped the company was beginning to regain momentum.

→ Eli Lilly’s Employer Push Could Unlock New GLP-1 Demand

Revenue and earnings both came in weaker than analysts had anticipated, reinforcing concerns that the payments giant is struggling to keep pace with an increasingly competitive fintech landscape.

Management also issued cautious guidance for the year ahead, further dampening enthusiasm for the stock.

→ The S&P 500's 3 Best-Performing Stocks So Far in 2026

Investors interpreted the outlook as a sign that PayPal is continuing to lose market share in certain areas where it once held undisputed dominance. If PayPal hopes to reignite its rally, May’s earnings report will need to show that growth is stabilizing and that management has a credible plan to regain the company’s competitive position.

title: Bank Of America Uses Zelle To Speed Charitable Giving And Deepen Ties
detail:
Bank Of America Uses Zelle To Speed Charitable Giving And Deepen Ties
Simply Wall St
Sun, March 15, 2026 at 4:07 PM CDT 4 min read
In this article:
BAC

Find your next quality investment with Simply Wall St's easy and powerful screener, trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.

Zelle and Bank of America have introduced near real time digital charitable disbursements from Donor Advised Funds to nonprofits.

The partnership replaces legacy paper based grant processes with faster, electronic transfers.

The new capability is designed to help nonprofits receive and use charitable funding more quickly and securely.

For investors watching NYSE:BAC, this move sits alongside a mixed recent share performance picture, with the stock at $46.72 and a 16.8% return over 1 year and 81.5% over 3 years. Over shorter periods, returns have been weaker, including a 3.9% decline over the past week and 11.1% over 30 days, along with a 16.5% decline year to date.

This Zelle collaboration provides another concrete data point on how Bank of America is using its digital platform for real world use cases in philanthropy. It also adds an angle on client engagement and social impact that investors can weigh alongside more traditional metrics such as share price, a value score of 5, and recent return trends when assessing the role of NYSE:BAC in a portfolio.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Bank of America by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Bank of America.

NYSE:BAC Earnings & Revenue Growth as at Mar 2026

5 things going right for Bank of America that this headline doesn't cover.

For Bank of America, extending Zelle into Donor Advised Fund disbursements is less about near term revenue and more about deepening high value relationships. DAFs are typically used by wealthier clients and institutions, so giving them faster, paper free grant payments ties this partnership directly into BAC’s private banking and wealth offerings, where peers such as JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley and Wells Fargo also compete hard for share. The proof of concept starting in Bank of America’s own Charitable Gift Fund lets the bank test client demand, operational reliability and fraud controls on a contained scale before any wider rollout. For you as an investor, this sits alongside existing data points on digital usage, AI tools like Erica and record engagement across payments. It adds another use case where digital infrastructure can support client stickiness and brand perception, particularly around social impact and philanthropy, at a time when the share price has been under short term pressure.

How This Fits Into The Bank of America Narrative

The Zelle DAF partnership supports the narrative that BAC’s digital engagement and AI powered services contribute to customer retention and fee potential, especially in wealth and investment management.

If adoption is slower than expected or if nonprofits and donors prefer existing processes, it could temper the idea that new digital offerings will consistently translate into stronger revenue growth.

The narrative focuses on digital banking, loan growth and interest rate management, and may not fully reflect how philanthropy focused products can shape BAC’s brand with high net worth clients and institutions.
